{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

Some scholars have raised objections to the use of odds ratios as 
an effect measurement [@sackett1996down; @norton2024requiem]. 

As we saw in [the figure comparing risk differences, risk ratios, and odds ratios](binary-outcome-associations.qmd#fig-rd-rr-or),
the odds ratio is not very closely correlated with the risk difference, 
and the risk difference is typically the metric 
that ultimately matters for policy decisions.

{{< slidebreak >}}

::: notes

Another objection is that odds ratios (and risk ratios, and risk differences) 
depend on the set of covariates
in a logistic regression model,
even when those covariates are independent of the exposure of interest
and do not interact with that exposure.
For example, consider the following model:

:::

$$\P(Y=y|X=x,C=c) = \red{\pi(x,c)}^y (1-\red{\pi(x,c)})^{1-y}$$

$$\red{\pi(x,c)} = \expitf{\eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_Cc}$$ {#eq-or-objections-expit-model}

Then:

$$
\ba
\Ef{Y|X=x} &= \Expf{\Ef{Y | X, C} | X=x}
&& \text{(law of total expectation)}
\\ &= \Expf{\red{\pi(X,C)} | X=x}
&& \text{(Bernoulli conditional mean: } \Ef{Y|X,C} = \pi(X,C) \text{)}
\\ &= \Expf{\expitf{\eta_0 + \b_X X + \b_C C} | X=x}
&& \text{(model definition of } \pi \text{)}
\\ &= \Expf{\expitf{\eta_0 + \b_X x + \b_C C} | X=x}
&& \text{(conditioning on } X = x \text{ fixes } X \text{ at } x \text{)}
\\ &= \int_c{\expitf{\eta_0 + \b_X x + \b_C c} ~\p(C=c|X=x)~ \partial c}
&& \text{(conditional expectation as an integral over } C \text{'s conditional distribution)}
\\ &= \int_c{\red{\pi(x,c)}~ \p(C=c|X=x)~ \partial c}
&& \text{(model definition of } \pi \text{, in reverse)}
\ea
$$

::: notes
Since the $\expitf{}$ function is nonlinear, 
we can't change the order of the expectation and $\expitf{}$ operators:
:::

$$\Expf{\expitf{\eta_0 + \b_XX + \b_CC} | X} \neq \expitf{\Expf{\eta_0 + \b_XX + \b_CC} | X}$$

{{< slidebreak >}}

In contrast, consider a model with an identity link function:

$$\P(Y=y|X=x,C=c) = \red{\pi(x,c)}^y (1-\red{\pi(x,c)})^{1-y}$$

$$\red{\pi(x,c)} = \eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_Cc$$ {#eq-or-objections-identity-model}

Then:

$$
\ba
\Ef{Y|X=x} &= \Expf{\Ef{Y | X, C} | X=x}
&& \text{(law of total expectation)}
\\ &= \Expf{\red{\pi(X,C)} | X=x}
&& \text{(Bernoulli conditional mean: } \Ef{Y|X,C} = \pi(X,C) \text{)}
\\ &= \Expf{\eta_0 + \b_X X + \b_C C | X=x}
&& \text{(identity-link model definition of } \pi \text{)}
\\ &= \Expf{\eta_0 | X=x} + \Expf{\b_X X | X=x} + \Expf{\b_C C | X=x}
&& \text{(linearity of expectation)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \Expf{\b_X X | X=x} + \Expf{\b_C C | X=x}
&& \text{(expectation of a constant)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \b_X \Expf{X | X=x} + \b_C \Expf{C | X=x}
&& \text{(constant factors move outside expectations)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \b_X x + \b_C \Expf{C | X=x}
&& \text{(} \Expf{X | X=x} = x \text{)}
\\ &= \red{(\eta_0 + \b_C\Ef{C | X=x})} + \teal{\b_Xx}
&& \text{(commute and regroup the terms)}
\ea
$$

If $C \ind X$, then $\Ef{C|X=x} = \Ef{C}$,
and we can simplify further:

$$
\ba
\Ef{Y|X=x} &= \red{(\eta_0 + \b_C \Ef{C|X=x})} + \teal{\b_Xx}
&& \text{(marginal mean under the identity link)}
\\
&= \red{(\eta_0 + \b_C \Ef{C})} + \teal{\b_Xx}
&& \text{(} C \ind X \text{, so } \Ef{C|X=x} = \Ef{C} \text{)}
\\&= \red{\eta_0^*}  + \teal{\b_Xx}
&& \text{(definition: } \eta_0^* \eqdef \eta_0 + \b_C \Ef{C} \text{)}
\ea
$$

Then:

$$
\ba
\deriv{x} \Ef{Y|X=x}
&= \deriv{x} \paren{\red{\eta_0^*} + \teal{\b_Xx}}
&& \text{(marginal mean when } C \ind X \text{)}
\\ &= \deriv{x}\eta_0^* + \deriv{x}\paren{\b_X x}
&& \text{(linearity of differentiation)}
\\ &= 0 + \deriv{x}\paren{\b_X x}
&& \text{(} \eta_0^* \text{ does not depend on } x \text{)}
\\ &= 0 + \b_X
&& \text{(derivative of a linear term)}
\\ &= \teal{\b_X}
&& \text{(additive identity)}
\ea
$$

The conditional slope is the same:

$$
\ba
\deriv{x} \Ef{Y|X=x,C=c}
&= \deriv{x} \paren{\eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_Cc}
&& \text{(identity-link model definition of } \pi \text{)}
\\ &= 0 + \b_X + 0
&& \text{(term-by-term: only } \b_X x \text{ depends on } x \text{)}
\\ &= \teal{\b_X}
&& \text{(additive identity)}
\ea
$$

Therefore:

$$
\deriv{x} \Ef{Y|X=x} = \teal{\b_X} = \deriv{x} \Ef{Y|X=x,C=c}
$$

In other words, for a model with an identity link function,
if covariates $X$ and $C$ are independent, 
then the slope with respect to $X$ doesn't depend on 
whether $C$ is included in the model
(and an analogous result holds if $X$ is discrete or categorical).

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#exr-marginal-model-non-indep}

What are the expressions for $\Ef{Y|X=x}$ and $\deriv{x} \Ef{Y|X=x}$
for the identity-link model in @eq-or-objections-identity-model,
if $\Ef{C|X=x} = \gamma_0 + \gamma_x x$?

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#sol-marginal-model-non-indep}

$$
\ba
\Ef{Y|X=x}
&= \Ef{\pi(x, C)|X=x}
&& \text{(law of total expectation, conditional on } X = x \text{)}
\\ &= \Ef{\eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_C C|X=x}
&& \text{(identity-link model)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_C \Ef{C|X=x}
&& \text{(linearity of expectation)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_C (\gamma_0 + \gamma_x x)
&& \text{(substitute } \Ef{C|X=x} = \gamma_0 + \gamma_x x \text{)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_C \gamma_0 + \b_C \gamma_x x
&& \text{(distribute)}
\\ &= (\eta_0 + \b_C \gamma_0) + (\b_X + \b_C \gamma_x) x
&& \text{(commute and regroup the terms)}
\ea
$$

$$
\ba
\deriv{x}\Ef{Y|X=x}
&= \deriv{x}\cb{(\eta_0 + \b_C \gamma_0) + (\b_X + \b_C \gamma_x) x}
&& \text{(marginal mean from the derivation of } \Ef{Y|X=x} \text{)}
\\ &= \deriv{x}(\eta_0 + \b_C \gamma_0) + \deriv{x}\cb{(\b_X + \b_C \gamma_x) x}
&& \text{(linearity of differentiation)}
\\ &= \b_X + \b_C \gamma_x
&& \text{(constants have derivative 0; derivative of a linear term)}
\ea
$$

The marginal mean is still linear in $x$,
but its slope $\b_X + \b_C \gamma_x$ differs from
the conditional slope $\b_X$
unless $\b_C = 0$ or $\gamma_x = 0$.

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#exr-marginal-model-non-indep-interaction}

What are the expressions for $\Ef{Y|X=x}$ and $\deriv{x} \Ef{Y|X=x}$,
if instead of the identity-link model in @eq-or-objections-identity-model,
$$\pi(x,c) = \eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_Cc +\b_{XC}xc$$
and $\Ef{C|X=x} = \gamma_0 + \gamma_x x$?


:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#sol-marginal-model-non-indep-interaction}

$$
\ba
\Ef{Y|X=x}
&= \Ef{\pi(x, C)|X=x}
&& \text{(law of total expectation, conditional on } X = x \text{)}
\\ &= \Ef{\eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_C C + \b_{XC}xC|X=x}
&& \text{(interaction model)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_C \Ef{C|X=x} + \b_{XC}x \Ef{C|X=x}
&& \text{(linearity of expectation)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \b_Xx + (\b_C + \b_{XC}x)\Ef{C|X=x}
&& \text{(factor out } \Ef{C|X=x} \text{)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \b_Xx + (\b_C + \b_{XC}x)(\gamma_0 + \gamma_x x)
&& \text{(substitute } \Ef{C|X=x} = \gamma_0 + \gamma_x x \text{)}
\\ &= \eta_0 + \b_Xx + \b_C\gamma_0 + \b_C\gamma_x x + \b_{XC}\gamma_0 x + \b_{XC}\gamma_x x^2
&& \text{(distribute)}
\\ &= (\eta_0 + \b_C\gamma_0) + (\b_X + \b_C\gamma_x + \b_{XC}\gamma_0) x + \b_{XC}\gamma_x x^2
&& \text{(commute and regroup the terms)}
\ea
$$

$$
\ba
\deriv{x}\Ef{Y|X=x}
&= \deriv{x}\cb{(\eta_0 + \b_C\gamma_0) + (\b_X + \b_C\gamma_x + \b_{XC}\gamma_0) x + \b_{XC}\gamma_x x^2}
&& \text{(marginal mean from the derivation of } \Ef{Y|X=x} \text{)}
\\ &= \deriv{x}(\eta_0 + \b_C\gamma_0) + \deriv{x}\cb{(\b_X + \b_C\gamma_x + \b_{XC}\gamma_0) x} + \deriv{x}\paren{\b_{XC}\gamma_x x^2}
&& \text{(linearity of differentiation)}
\\ &= (\b_X + \b_C\gamma_x + \b_{XC}\gamma_0) + 2\b_{XC}\gamma_x x
&& \text{(constants have derivative 0; derivative of a linear term; power rule)}
\ea
$$

So, answering the hint:
yes — adding the interaction term changes the functional form of $\Ef{Y|X=x}$.
The marginal mean is now quadratic in $x$
(whenever $\b_{XC} \neq 0$ and $\gamma_x \neq 0$),
even though the conditional model is linear in $x$ for each fixed $c$,
and the marginal slope now varies with $x$.

:::
